articles.title: Charity
articles.author: Grace Ingles Frost
articles.complete_text:
"Neither do I condemn thee; go and sin no more" (John 8:11).

Charity! Charity!
Not like the Pharisees of old,
Who close about his form did draw
His garment, lest perchance a fold
Should unto him unhallowed be
From contact with a fellow man
Less fortunate than he.

Charity! Charity!
Like that which forth from lips divine
As precious pearls did flow,
When she, that erring one, was brought,
Her shame, her guilt to show.
The words, "Let him who hath no sin
Be first to cast a stone,"
And lo! the accusers, one by one,
Stole forth and left alone
The woman with her God.

Then through the ominous silence broke
A thought of wondrous love,
Which dormant good in heart awoke,
That such grave stigma bore,
"Neither do I condemn thee:
Go and sin no more."
articles.notes: Complete poem with clear title, author attribution, and biblical theme consistent with Relief Society Magazine content

articles.title: Invocation
articles.complete_text:
God give us loyal hearts and brave,
Great souls who walk by love,
Not afraid
To hurl the contumelious stone
Upon another,
But ever eager, glad to save;
Fling forth the life-line wide,
To rescue from the storm-tossed wave
The sister or the brother.
articles.notes: Short poem with religious theme, appears to be a prayer or invocation, consistent with magazine's spiritual content

misc_summary: The remaining content consists of legitimate miscellaneous material including: front cover information, table of contents, advertisers' directory with local Salt Lake City businesses, General Board notes about Relief Society activities and personnel changes, obituary notice for Mrs. Marilla Miller Daniels, information about German genealogy book publication, and various advertisements for local businesses and services.
